title: Ranunculus hemicyclodon
taxonomy: Ranunculus hemicyclodon is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup or ranunculus family. This family is part of the larger order Ranunculales, which also includes other families like the Circaeanaceae and the Thalictraceae. The Ranunculaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Ranunculoideae and the Helleoroideae. Within the family Ranunculaceae, Ranunculus hemicyclodon is classified as a member of the subfamily Ranunculoideae. This classification is based on shared characteristics and genetic relationships among the plant species within the family.

intro: Ranunculus hemicyclodon, also known as Hemicyclodon, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae), native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Hemicyclodon include iridoid glycosides, sesquiterpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often employed to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
